narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: burning fossil fuels on an international flight rather than attending remotely and attending to pressing issues at home.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The quote directly criticizes the mayor's decision to travel to a climate summit, framing it as hypocritical and ineffective, questioning the practical impact and wisdom of the policy action (attending the summit) in light of its carbon cost and opportunity cost regarding local issues.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: White Garcetti was away, Los Angeles continued to struggle with the high crime and homelessness that have come to define his legacy, as well as a crisis in the City Council triggered by racist comments from City Council members within Garcetti’s party.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement criticizes Mayor Garcetti's competence and priorities by highlighting domestic crises (crime, homelessness, political scandal) that persisted or worsened while he was abroad, challenging his effectiveness and integrity as a leader.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: It is not clear why the conference could not be held remotely.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement implicitly criticizes the climate movement's practices by suggesting its key actors (mayors at a summit) are not employing readily available, low-carbon alternatives (remote attendance), thereby questioning the movement's operational integrity and commitment to its stated goals.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: burning fossil fuels on an international flight rather than attending remotely and attending to pressing issues at home.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The quote directly questions the effectiveness and logic of the mayor's climate action by highlighting the carbon cost of his travel and suggesting he should have prioritized local problems, thereby criticizing the implementation and impact of the policy.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: though L.A.’s infrastructure is not widely considered “high-quality.” A proposed high-speed rail between L.A. and San Francisco has been largely canceled, and current passenger rail service from L.A. to San Diego has been suspended
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote challenges the competence and credibility of the mayor and his administration by pointing out failures and shortcomings in the city's transportation infrastructure, undermining their authority on climate and urban policy.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Garcetti’s “Green New Deal” calls for shuttering natural gas plants; it has been opposed by public works unions, and lost the only serious electoral challenge it faced in a 2019 city council race.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The quote criticizes a specific climate policy initiative by highlighting opposition from unions and electoral failure, which challenges the movement's practical support and political viability.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: That is one-fourth (economy) or one-half (premium) the global average per capita use of carbon per year. It is not clear why the conference could not be held remotely.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: By quantifying the flight's emissions as a fraction of an annual average and questioning the necessity of in-person attendance, the text minimizes the significance of the individual action in the context of the broader climate crisis.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: burning fossil fuels on an international flight rather than attending remotely and attending to pressing issues at home.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text directly criticizes the mayor's decision to travel to a climate summit, framing it as hypocritical and ineffective, questioning the practical impact and motives of the climate policy action.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote criticizes Mayor Garcetti's competence and priorities by highlighting domestic crises he is allegedly neglecting, thereby challenging the integrity and effectiveness of his leadership in relation to climate and other civic duties.
